definitions: The state or quality of a material or membrane that allows liquids or gases to pass through it.
The ability of a rock or other material to transmit fluids through pores and fissures.
The measure of the ability of a material to support the formation of a magnetic field within itself.
examples: The permeability of the soil affects drainage.
Membrane permeability is crucial for cell function.
Magnetic permeability varies among different metals.
